message:
fix(3d): Element-based atom coloring for ball-and-stick and surface modes

Previously, atoms in 3D protein views were displayed with uniform colors:
- Ball-and-stick mode had dark emissive settings that washed out element colors
- Surface mode used a static light blue color for all atoms regardless of element

This commit fixes both rendering modes to properly display element-based colors:

ELEMENT_COLORS map expanded:
- Added 8 new elements: BR, I, F, B, SI, AL, CO, NI
- Carbon color darkened (#64748b) for better contrast with other elements
- All colors now optimized for dark backgrounds using CPK color scheme

Ball-and-stick mode (buildBallAndStick):
- Removed dark emissive color that was overpowering element colors
- Set emissiveIntensity to 0 to let element colors render accurately
- Reduced shininess from 140 to 80 for more natural appearance
- Changed bond color to neutral gray (#9ca3af) to not compete with atoms

Surface mode (buildSurfaceImpostor):
- Completely rewrote to use element-based colors via vertexColors
- Outer surface: semi-transparent with element colors per atom
- Inner core: brightened element colors (lerped 30% toward white) for depth
- Fixed memory allocation: reuse Color objects instead of creating in loop

The result is protein structures that now show meaningful chemical information
through color - nitrogen (blue), oxygen (red), sulfur (yellow), etc. - rather
than uniform coloring that provided no structural insight.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
